name: kravhantering
description: En webbapplikation för kravhantering som stödjer företagets kravmodell och kravprocess
author: Viscalyx

scripts.dev:fresh: npm run kill:port && npm run clean && npm run dev
scripts.idp:reset: docker compose -f docker-compose.idp.yml down --remove-orphans && docker compose -f docker-compose.idp.yml up -d --force-recreate idp
scripts.build:local-prod: dotenv -e .env.prodlike -- cross-env NODE_ENV=production BUILD_TARGET=local-prod next build
scripts.start:prodlike: npm run build:local-prod && dotenv -e .env.prodlike -- cross-env NODE_ENV=production BUILD_TARGET=local-prod next start --hostname 127.0.0.1 --port 3001
scripts.check: npm run type-check && npm run lint:py && npm run dotenv:check && npm run format:check && npm run spell:check && npm run lint && npm run lint:md && npm run test
scripts.fix: npm run format && npm run lint:fix && npm run lint:md:fix && npm run dotenv:fix && npm run spell:fix
scripts.test:integration:all: npm run test:integration && npm run test:integration:prodlike
scripts.generate:guide: npm run kill:port && npm run db:setup && playwright test --config playwright.guide.config.ts
scripts.clean:all: rimraf .next out && echo 'Run npm run db:setup to restore the local SQL Server database'
scripts.purge:install: rimraf node_modules && npm cache clean --force && npm install && rimraf package-lock.json && npm install

//purge:install: Two-phase install: first install rebuilds node_modules with a (possibly corrupt) lockfile,
then the lockfile is deleted and regenerated with node_modules present.
This works around an npm bug where platform-specific optional deps (e.g. @esbuild/openharmony-arm64)
are written as 'extraneous' instead of 'optional' when node_modules is absent during resolution,
which causes 'npm ci' to fail with EBADPLATFORM in CI.
//overrides: Overrides pin transitive dependencies that still resolve to vulnerable versions.
Remove each override when the parent dependency updates to include the fix natively.
Document below this. Example: @swc/helpers: next@16.1.6 pins 0.5.15, but next-intl's @swc/core@^1.15.2 peer-requires >=0.5.17. Override to 0.5.19 resolves the conflict.
hono: @modelcontextprotocol/sdk@1.29.0 pulls hono through hono@^4.11.4 and @hono/node-server@1.19.14 -> hono@^4. Override maps vulnerable hono <4.12.18 to 4.12.18.
@hono/node-server: @modelcontextprotocol/sdk@1.29.0 pulls @hono/node-server@1.19.14 via @hono/node-server@^1.19.9. Keep the override at 1.19.14 until the SDK parent no longer needs it.

uuid: mssql@12.5.2 resolves tedious@19.2.1 -> @azure/identity@4.13.1 -> @azure/msal-node@5.1.4 -> uuid@14.0.0 through the override, and typeorm@0.3.29 resolves uuid@14.0.0 through the override. Override maps vulnerable uuid ranges to 14.0.0.
postcss: next@16.2.6 pins postcss@8.4.31, while the lockfile resolves postcss@8.5.14 through the override. Override maps the vulnerable Next postcss pin to 8.5.14.
ip-address: @modelcontextprotocol/sdk@1.29.0 resolves express-rate-limit@8.4.1 -> ip-address@10.2.0 through the override. Override maps vulnerable ip-address@10.1.0 to 10.2.0.
fast-uri: @modelcontextprotocol/sdk@1.29.0 resolves ajv@8.20.0 -> fast-uri@3.1.2 through the override. Override maps vulnerable fast-uri <=3.1.1 to 3.1.2.
